div1.entryFree: Fleer, vb. to make a wry face, to grin ("to make an evil countenance with the mouth by uncovering of the teeth", cf. Halliwell, dictionary of archaisms, I, 361): one rubbed his elbow thus, and --ed and swore a better speech was never spoke before, LLL V, 2, 109. a man that is no --ing tell-tale, Caes. I, 3, 117. With at: never f. and jest at me, Ado V, 1, 58. to f. and scorn at our solemnity, Rom. I, 5, 59.
